areaConverter.description: Free land area converter for Indian real estate. Convert Gaj to Acre, Sq Ft to Gaj, Bigha to Acre, Ground to Acre, and more. Instant and accurate area unit conversion for property buyers in India.
areaConverter.keywords: area converter india, sq ft to gaj, gaj to acre, bigha to acre, 4840 gaj in acre, 360 sq ft in gaj, 2400 gaj in acre, 2400 sq ft in gaj, bigha to acre calculator, bigha to acre conversion, gaj to bigha, 43560 sq ft to ground, ground to acre, land calculator india, bigha in acre, sq ft to gaj converter, gaj to sq ft, bigha converter, 1 acre to bigha in haryana, jamin calculator, 360 sq yard to gaj, land area converter India, acre to bigha, square feet to gaj, 1 gaj in bigha, 360ghar

q1.question: How do I convert square feet to square meters?
q1.answer: Multiply square feet by 0.0929. For example, 1000 sq ft = 92.9 sq m. Use our area converter for instant, accurate results.

q2.question: What is 1 bigha in square feet?
q2.answer: 1 bigha equals approximately 27,000 sq ft in Haryana and 27,220 sq ft in Uttar Pradesh. Bigha varies by state — always verify the local measurement standard.

q3.question: How many square feet in 1 kanal?
q3.answer: 1 kanal equals 5,445 sq ft. This is standard across Punjab, Haryana, and Himachal Pradesh.

q4.question: What is the difference between carpet area and built-up area?
q4.answer: Carpet area is the net usable floor area inside walls. Built-up area includes carpet area plus walls and balconies. Super built-up area adds common areas like corridors and lobbies.

q5.question: How do I convert Gaj to Acre?
q5.answer: 1 Gaj equals 9 sq ft, and 1 Acre equals 43,560 sq ft. So to convert Gaj to Acre, divide the number of Gaj by 4,840. For example, 4,840 Gaj = 1 Acre, and 2,400 Gaj = 0.496 Acre. Use the converter above for instant results.

q6.question: How do I convert Bigha to Acre?
q6.answer: 1 Bigha in Haryana equals approximately 27,000 sq ft, which is about 0.6198 Acre (27,000 ÷ 43,560). In Uttar Pradesh, 1 Bigha ≈ 0.625 Acre. Since Bigha varies by state, use our converter to get accurate results for your region.

q7.question: How do I convert Square Feet to Gaj?
q7.answer: 1 Gaj = 9 sq ft. To convert sq ft to Gaj, divide the square feet value by 9. For example, 360 sq ft = 40 Gaj, and 2,400 sq ft = 266.67 Gaj. The converter above calculates this instantly.

q8.question: How many Gaj are in 1 Acre?
q8.answer: 1 Acre = 43,560 sq ft = 4,840 Gaj (since 1 Gaj = 9 sq ft). So if you see a plot listed as 4,840 Gaj, it equals exactly 1 Acre.

q9.question: How do I convert Ground to Acre?
q9.answer: 1 Ground = 2,400 sq ft. Since 1 Acre = 43,560 sq ft, 1 Ground equals approximately 0.0551 Acre (2,400 ÷ 43,560). Ground is commonly used in South India, especially Tamil Nadu.

step1.name: Select input unit
step1.text: Choose the unit you want to convert from — sq ft, sq m, bigha, kanal, marla, acre, hectare, or others.

step2.name: Enter the area value
step2.text: Type the numeric value of the area you want to convert.

step3.name: Select output unit
step3.text: Choose the unit you want to convert to. The result updates instantly.

step4.name: Copy or note the result
step4.text: The converted value is displayed immediately. You can copy it for use in property documents or calculations.

areaConverter.heroDesc: Convert between Indian land measurement units instantly — Gaj to Acre, Sq Ft to Gaj, Bigha to Acre, Ground to Acre, and more. Free and accurate area conversion for property buyers and sellers.
areaConverter.gajToAcreDesc: Gaj (also called Square Yard) is one of the most commonly used land measurement units in North India, especially in Haryana, Punjab, and Delhi NCR. 1 Gaj equals 9 square feet, and 1 Acre equals 4,840 Gaj (43,560 sq ft). To convert Gaj to Acre, divide the Gaj value by 4,840. For example, 4,840 Gaj equals 1 Acre, and 2,400 Gaj equals approximately 0.496 Acre. This conversion is essential when comparing plot sizes listed in Gaj with agricultural land measured in Acres. Use the converter above for instant, accurate results.
areaConverter.sqFtToGajDesc: Converting square feet to Gaj is one of the most frequent calculations in Indian real estate, especially in Haryana and Uttar Pradesh. Since 1 Gaj equals 9 square feet, you simply divide the square feet value by 9. For example, 360 sq ft equals 40 Gaj, 2,400 sq ft equals 266.67 Gaj, and 43,560 sq ft (1 Acre) equals 4,840 Gaj. Property listings in Gurugram, Faridabad, and other NCR cities often use Gaj, making this conversion crucial for buyers comparing plot sizes across different listings and portals.
areaConverter.headingDesc: Compare properties easily by converting between local and standard measurement units.

areaCalculator.title: Carpet Area Calculator - Carpet to Built-up & RERA Area Conversion | 360Ghar
areaCalculator.description: Free carpet area calculator to convert between carpet area, built-up area, and super built-up area. Calculate RERA carpet area, convert MOFA to RERA, and understand loading factor for properties in India. Accurate area conversion in square feet.
areaCalculator.keywords: carpet area calculator, carpet area to built up area calculator, mofa to rera carpet conversion calculator, rera carpet area calculator, super built up area to carpet area calculator, carpet area to built-up area calculator India, built up area to carpet area calculator, carpet area in square feet calculator, how to calculate carpet area from super built up area, carpet area calculation formula, carpet area for flat calculator, loading factor calculator, carpet area vs built up area, RERA carpet area, carpet area converter, area calculation formula India, flat carpet area calculator, built up area vs carpet area, carpet area to super built up area calculator, carpet area kya hota hai, 360ghar

q1.question: How do I calculate the area of a room?
q1.answer: Measure length and width in feet or meters, then multiply them. Area = Length × Width. For irregular shapes, break the room into rectangles and add the areas.

q2.question: What is carpet area?
q2.answer: Carpet area is the net usable floor area inside the walls of an apartment. It excludes walls, balconies, and common areas. RERA mandates that developers disclose carpet area.

q3.question: How is super built-up area calculated?
q3.answer: Super built-up area = Built-up area + Proportionate common area. The loading factor (usually 25-35%) is applied to carpet area to arrive at super area.

q4.question: What is the difference between RERA carpet area and usable area?
q4.answer: RERA carpet area includes the area enclosed by walls (up to the inner face). Usable area may exclude some wall portions. RERA definition is more generous than the older 'carpet area' standard.

q5.question: How do I convert carpet area to built-up area?
q5.answer: To convert carpet area to built-up area, add 10-15% to the carpet area. The formula is: Built-up Area = Carpet Area × 1.10 to 1.15. This accounts for walls, balconies, and exclusive terraces. For example, if carpet area is 1000 sq ft, built-up area is approximately 1100-1150 sq ft.

q6.question: What is MOFA to RERA carpet area conversion?
q6.answer: Under MOFA (Maharashtra Ownership Flats Act), carpet area excluded internal partition walls. RERA carpet area includes the area within internal walls, making it about 5-8% larger than MOFA carpet area. For example, a flat with MOFA carpet area of 1000 sq ft would have approximately 1050-1080 sq ft RERA carpet area. Use our calculator to convert accurately.

q7.question: How to calculate RERA carpet area?
q7.answer: RERA carpet area is the net usable floor area of an apartment, measured from the inner face of walls. It includes internal partition walls but excludes external walls, common areas, balconies, and open terraces. Formula: RERA Carpet Area = Total Floor Area − External Walls − Balconies − Open Terraces − Common Areas.

q8.question: How to convert carpet area to square feet?
q8.answer: If carpet area is given in square meters, multiply by 10.764 to get square feet. If in square yards (gaj), multiply by 9. For example, 100 sq m carpet area = 1076.4 sq ft. Use our free area converter tool for other unit conversions like bigha, kanal, or marla to square feet.

q9.question: How to calculate carpet area for a flat?
q9.answer: To calculate carpet area for a flat, measure each room's length × width from the inner face of walls, then sum all rooms. Exclude balconies, external walls, service shafts, and open terraces. Alternatively, if you know the super built-up area and loading factor, use: Carpet Area = Super Built-up Area × (1 − Loading Factor/100).

step1.name: Measure room dimensions
step1.text: Measure the length and width of each room in feet or meters using a measuring tape or laser distance meter.

step2.name: Calculate carpet area
step2.text: Multiply length × width for each room. Add all room areas to get total carpet area.

step3.name: Add walls for built-up area
step3.text: Add 10-15% to carpet area for walls and balconies to get built-up area.

step4.name: Calculate super built-up area
step4.text: Add the loading factor (25-35%) to built-up area to get super built-up area. This includes common areas like corridors and lobbies.

areaCalculator.builtUpLoadingHint: Extra over carpet for walls & balconies (default 15%)
areaCalculator.carpetDesc: This is the actual area you can use within the walls.
areaCalculator.loadingAreaHint: The portion of super area that is not usable carpet space.
areaCalculator.carpetAreaDesc: The area enclosed within the walls, actual usable floor area. According to RERA, it excludes external walls, service shafts, balconies, and open terraces.
areaCalculator.builtUpAreaDesc: Carpet Area + Area of walls + Area of balconies. This is typically 10-15% more than the carpet area.
areaCalculator.superBuiltUpAreaDesc: Built-up Area + Proportionate share of common areas (lobby, lifts, stairs, amenities). This is what you usually pay for.
areaCalculator.heroDesc: Convert between Carpet Area, Built-up Area, and Super Built-up Area instantly. RERA-compliant calculations with loading factor for properties in India.
areaCalculator.carpetToBuiltUpTitle: Carpet Area to Built-up Area Conversion
areaCalculator.carpetToBuiltUpDesc: Converting carpet area to built-up area is essential when comparing property sizes. Built-up area includes the carpet area plus the area covered by walls and balconies. Typically, built-up area is 10-15% more than carpet area. For example, a flat with 1000 sq ft carpet area will have approximately 1100-1150 sq ft built-up area. When a builder quotes a super built-up area, the carpet area (your actual usable space) is calculated by subtracting the loading factor — usually 25-35% — from the super built-up area.
areaCalculator.reraVsMofaTitle: RERA Carpet Area vs MOFA Carpet Area
areaCalculator.reraVsMofaDesc: Before RERA, Maharashtra followed MOFA (Maharashtra Ownership Flats Act), which defined carpet area as the area excluding internal partition walls. RERA expanded the definition to include the area within internal walls, making RERA carpet area approximately 5-8% larger than MOFA carpet area. This difference matters because homebuyers paying per square foot get more usable space under the RERA definition. If a builder quotes MOFA carpet area of 1000 sq ft, the RERA equivalent would be roughly 1050-1080 sq ft. Always verify which standard is being used when comparing property prices.

loanEligibility.title: Home Loan Eligibility Calculator India | Check Max Loan Amount | 360Ghar
loanEligibility.description: Check your Home Loan Eligibility instantly. Calculate the maximum loan amount you can get based on your salary, existing EMIs, and tenure. Plan your home purchase budget.
loanEligibility.keywords: home loan eligibility calculator India, how much home loan can I get, housing loan eligibility check, salary based home loan calculator, 360ghar financial tools

q1.question: How much home loan can I get on my salary?
q1.answer: Most banks offer 60 times your monthly income as the maximum loan amount. For a ₹1 lakh/month salary, you may qualify for ₹60 lakhs. Your existing EMIs, credit score, and property value also affect eligibility.

q2.question: What is the FOIR for home loans?
q2.answer: FOIR (Fixed Obligation to Income Ratio) is the percentage of income used for debt repayment. Banks typically allow 40-50% FOIR. Lower FOIR means higher loan eligibility.

q3.question: Does credit score affect loan eligibility?
q3.answer: Yes. A CIBIL score above 750 increases eligibility and gets better interest rates. Scores below 650 may result in loan rejection or higher rates.

q4.question: Can I increase my home loan eligibility?
q4.answer: Yes. Add a co-applicant with income, close existing loans, increase loan tenure, or improve your credit score. A higher down payment also reduces the required loan amount.

step1.text: Enter your gross monthly income including salary, business income, and any other regular income sources.

step2.name: Add existing obligations
step2.text: Enter your current EMIs, credit card payments, and any other fixed monthly debt obligations.

step3.name: Check the eligibility result
step3.text: The calculator shows your maximum eligible loan amount based on FOIR, income multiplier, and other factors.

step4.name: Compare with property value
step4.text: Banks fund 75-90% of property value. Ensure your eligible amount covers the required portion after your down payment.

propertyChecklist.keywords: property document checklist India, documents for buying flat, home loan documents list, property legal verification checklist, real estate documents India, 360ghar guide

q1.question: What documents are needed to buy a flat?
q1.answer: Key documents include sale deed, property registration, encumbrance certificate, tax receipts, building approval plan, occupancy certificate, and society transfer papers. Our checklist covers 40+ documents.

q2.question: What is an encumbrance certificate?
q2.answer: An encumbrance certificate (EC) records all financial transactions on a property — mortgages, liens, and transfers. It proves the property has a clear title. Get it from the sub-registrar office.

q3.question: Do I need a lawyer for property purchase?
q3.answer: While not legally required, hiring a property lawyer is strongly recommended for title verification, agreement review, and due diligence. The cost (₹10,000-50,000) is negligible compared to the property value at risk.

q4.question: What is an occupancy certificate?
q4.answer: An occupancy certificate (OC) is issued by the local authority confirming the building is complete and fit for occupation. Without OC, the property may be illegal and face demolition risk.

step3.name: Track document status

q1.question: What is Vastu Shastra?
q1.answer: Vastu Shastra is an ancient Indian science of architecture that guides building orientation, room placement, and spatial arrangement for positive energy flow. It combines earth's magnetic fields, sun direction, and cosmic energy principles.

q2.question: Which direction should the main entrance face?
q2.answer: North, northeast, or east-facing entrances are considered auspicious in Vastu. South-facing entrances are generally avoided but can be corrected with remedies.

q3.question: Is Vastu important for apartments?
q3.answer: Yes, Vastu principles apply to apartments too. Key factors include entrance direction, kitchen placement (southeast), master bedroom (southwest), and pooja room (northeast).

q4.question: Can Vastu defects be corrected without demolition?
q4.answer: Yes. Many Vastu defects can be corrected using mirrors, colors, metals, pyramids, and specific placement of objects. Professional Vastu consultants can suggest non-structural remedies.

q5.question: Is Vastu Shastra scientifically proven?
q5.answer: While Vastu Shastra is rooted in traditional Indian knowledge, many of its principles align with environmental science — natural light, ventilation, and spatial planning. It is widely followed in India as a cultural and architectural practice rather than a strict science.

q6.question: How to check Vastu of house online?
q6.answer: You can check Vastu of your house online for free using 360Ghar's AI Vastu checker. Simply upload a photo or screenshot of your floor plan, mark the north direction, and the AI will analyze room placements, entrance direction, kitchen position, and other Vastu factors. You receive a compliance score out of 10 along with room-by-room analysis and practical remedies — all in under a minute, without any on-site visit.

q7.question: How to check Vastu for flat or apartment?
q7.answer: For flats and apartments, Vastu analysis focuses on the unit's entrance direction, kitchen placement (ideally southeast), master bedroom (southwest), and toilet positions. Upload your apartment's floor plan to 360Ghar's Vastu checker, mark the north direction, and get an instant AI-powered analysis. Since structural changes are limited in flats, the tool emphasizes non-structural remedies like color adjustments, mirror placements, and elemental corrections.

q8.question: Is there a free online Vastu calculator?
q8.answer: Yes, 360Ghar offers a free online Vastu calculator that analyzes your floor plan using AI. Unlike simple direction-based calculators, it evaluates the complete layout — room positions, entrance orientation, kitchen and toilet placements — and generates a Vastu compliance score with specific recommendations. There is no charge and no sign-up required.

q9.question: What are common Vastu dosh and their remedies?
q9.answer: Common Vastu dosh include south-facing entrance, kitchen in the northeast, toilet in the northeast or center, and master bedroom in the northeast. Remedies vary by dosh: a south entrance can be corrected with a copper swastik or pyramid; a northeast kitchen may need color corrections and Vastu salt; toilet dosh can be balanced with metal strips and specific colors. 360Ghar's Vastu checker identifies these dosh automatically and suggests tailored remedies for each.

step1.name: Upload your floor plan
step1.text: Upload an image or photo of your property's floor plan. Our AI will analyze the layout.

step2.name: Mark the compass directions
step2.text: Ensure the floor plan shows north direction correctly. The analysis depends on accurate orientation.

step3.name: Review Vastu analysis
step3.text: The AI checker identifies Vastu-compliant and non-compliant zones in your floor plan.

step4.name: Apply Vastu remedies
step4.text: For non-compliant areas, the tool suggests remedies like color changes, mirror placement, or room reassignment.

vastu.vastuFlatVsHouseTitle: Vastu for Flats vs Independent Houses
vastu.vastuFlatVsHouseDesc: Vastu Shastra applies to both flats and independent houses, but the evaluation priorities differ. For independent houses, you have full control over plot orientation, entrance placement, and room positioning. For apartments and flats, you share the building's orientation and must work within the builder's layout. Key Vastu factors for flats include the apartment's entrance direction, kitchen position relative to the unit's center, master bedroom location, and toilet placements. While you cannot change the structural layout of a flat, our Vastu checker identifies non-compliant areas and suggests practical remedies like color adjustments, mirror placements, and elemental corrections that do not require demolition.

rentReceipt.title: Rent Receipt Generator for HRA Claim | Free PDF Download | 360Ghar
rentReceipt.description: Generate rent receipts for HRA tax exemption claims instantly. Free online rent receipt maker with landlord PAN, revenue stamp, monthly & full-year batch generation. Download PDF for Income Tax submission.
rentReceipt.keywords: rent receipt generator, HRA receipt, rent receipt for tax exemption, rent receipt download, generate rent receipt online, monthly rent receipt, HRA claim receipt, landlord PAN rent receipt, revenue stamp rent receipt, rent receipt PDF, kiraya rasid, किराया रसीद, rent receipt India
rentReceipt.headingDesc: Generate valid rent receipts for HRA tax exemption claims. Download as PDF or print instantly. Free, no signup required.
rentReceipt.stampStateNote: The ₹5,000 revenue stamp threshold varies by state under the Indian Stamp Act. Some states (e.g., Maharashtra, Karnataka) have different limits or surcharges. Confirm the applicable stamp duty rule for your state before submitting receipts for HRA claims.
rentReceipt.panWarning: PAN is mandatory if annual rent exceeds ₹1,00,000
rentReceipt.addressPlaceholder: e.g., Flat 402, Tower B, DLF Phase 1, Gurugram
rentReceipt.revenueStampNotice: Cash payment exceeds ₹5,000 — a revenue stamp of ₹1 must be affixed on the printed receipt as per the Indian Stamp Act.
rentReceipt.tipMetro: For metro cities (Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata, Chennai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, Ahmedabad), HRA exemption is 50% of basic salary. For non-metro cities, it is 40%.
rentReceipt.tipPAN: If your annual rent exceeds ₹1,00,000, you must provide your landlord's PAN to your employer. If the landlord doesn't have a PAN, a written declaration is required.
rentReceipt.tipStamp: A ₹1 revenue stamp is mandatory on rent receipts for cash payments exceeding ₹5,000 under the Indian Stamp Act. No stamp needed for UPI, bank transfer, or cheque payments.
rentReceipt.tipDocs: Along with rent receipts, keep rental agreement, bank statements showing rent transfers, and landlord PAN (if applicable) ready for HRA claim verification.
rentReceipt.faqHeading: Frequently Asked Questions about Rent Receipts & HRA
